cards.title: Adaptogens — Evidence & Clinical Guide

alerts.label: Definition (Brekhman & Dardymov, 1969)
alerts.text: An adaptogen must: (1) be non-toxic, (2) produce a non-specific resistance to stress, (3) have a normalising effect regardless of direction of pathological change — i.e. bidirectional modulation.

rows: ↑ CNS stimulation, ↑ NK cells, ↑ NO, antidiabetic

pearls.label: 🟡 Avoid adaptogens in
pearls.text: Pregnancy (most), autoimmune disease on immunosuppressants (ginseng, eleuthero stimulate immunity), hyperthyroidism (ashwagandha ↑ T3/T4), hormone-sensitive cancers (Panax ginseng has oestrogen-like activity). Rhodiola is stimulating — avoid in evening, bipolar, or with SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk at high dose).

cards._legacyHtml:
Definition (Brekhman & Dardymov, 1969)
An adaptogen must: (1) be non-toxic, (2) produce a non-specific resistance to stress, (3) have a normalising effect regardless of direction of pathological change — i.e. bidirectional modulation.
AdaptogenLatin NamePrimary ActionsBest EvidenceDose
AshwagandhaWithania somnifera↓ Cortisol, ↑ thyroid (T3/T4), ↑ testosterone, ↑ muscleAnxiety ★, fatigue, hypothyroid, male fertilityKSM-66: 300–600mg/day
Rhodiola roseaRhodiola rosea↑ Serotonin/dopamine, ↓ cortisol, ↑ mental performanceBurnout ★, mental fatigue, depression adjunctSHR-5: 200–400mg/day (3% rosavins)
Panax ginsengPanax ginseng↑ CNS stimulation, ↑ NK cells, ↑ NO, antidiabeticCognitive function ★, T2DM, fatigue, immune200–400mg (standardised 4% ginsenosides)
EleutheroEleutherococcus senticosus↑ Physical endurance, immune modulationAthletic performance, chronic fatigue300–1200mg/day
SchisandraSchisandra chinensisHepatoprotective, ↓ cortisol, ↑ mental enduranceLiver protection, stress, cognitive500–2000mg/day
Holy basil (Tulsi)Ocimum tenuiflorum↓ Cortisol, antibacterial, adaptogenic, ↓ blood sugarAnxiety, metabolic syndrome, BNYS-specific ★300–2000mg or 1–2 cups/day
🟡 Avoid adaptogens in
Pregnancy (most), autoimmune disease on immunosuppressants (ginseng, eleuthero stimulate immunity), hyperthyroidism (ashwagandha ↑ T3/T4), hormone-sensitive cancers (Panax ginseng has oestrogen-like activity). Rhodiola is stimulating — avoid in evening, bipolar, or with SSRIs (serotonin syndrome risk at high dose).

cards.title: Herb-Drug Interactions — Critical Table

pearls.label: 🟡 Rule of thumb
pearls.text: ANY herb with antiplatelet activity (garlic, ginger, turmeric, ginkgo, dong quai, fish oil >3g) → withhold 2 weeks before surgery. Always ask about herbs BEFORE prescribing. St John's Wort interacts with more drugs than any pharmaceutical except rifampicin — it must be avoided with any narrow therapeutic index drug.

cards._legacyHtml:
High-risk combinations — know these
HerbDrug ClassInteractionMechanismRisk
St John's WortSSRIs, SNRIs↑ Serotonin syndrome↑ SerotoninHIGH ★
St John's WortWarfarin, OCP, HIV meds, cyclosporin↓ Drug levels → treatment failureCYP3A4 + P-gp inductionHIGH ★
Ginkgo bilobaWarfarin, aspirin, NSAIDs↑ Bleeding riskAntiplatelet effectHIGH ★
ValerianBenzodiazepines, barbiturates↑ CNS depressionGABA potentiationModerate
Garlic / Dong quaiWarfarin, antiplatelets↑ Bleeding riskAntiplatelet + anticoagulantModerate
Liquorice (DGL-free)Antihypertensives, corticosteroids↑ BP, ↓ K⁺, potentiates steroidsPseudoaldosteronism (glycyrrhizin)Moderate
EchinaceaImmunosuppressants (cyclosporin, tacrolimus)↓ Drug efficacyImmune stimulation opposesModerate
AshwagandhaThyroid hormones↑ T3/T4 → possible thyrotoxicosisTSH-lowering, T4→T3 conversion ↑Moderate
🟡 Rule of thumb
ANY herb with antiplatelet activity (garlic, ginger, turmeric, ginkgo, dong quai, fish oil >3g) → withhold 2 weeks before surgery. Always ask about herbs BEFORE prescribing. St John's Wort interacts with more drugs than any pharmaceutical except rifampicin — it must be avoided with any narrow therapeutic index drug.

pearls.label: 🟡 Herb Safety Before Surgery
pearls.text: Stop all herbal medicines 2 weeks before elective surgery. Garlic, ginger, ginkgo, ginseng — antiplatelet effects. Hawthorn — cardiac effects. St John's Wort — CYP induction affects anaesthetic metabolism. Valerian, kava — potentiate anaesthetic agents. Always take a full herbal history at pre-operative assessment — most patients do not volunteer this information unless specifically asked.

Boswellia serrata5-LOX inhibitor, reduces LTB4, potent anti-inflammatory300-400mg TDS (65% boswellic acids)Good for OA, asthma, IBD, RAAnticoagulants (mild)
Turmeric (Curcumin)NF-kB inhibition, COX inhibition, antioxidant500-1000mg with piperine 20mg BDGood — broad anti-inflammatory evidenceWarfarin at high doses
Willow bark (Salicin)COX inhibition (aspirin-like), antipyretic, analgesic240mg salicin/dayGood for LBP, OA painAspirin, NSAIDs, anticoagulants — additive
Ginger (Zingiber officinale)COX-1/2 inhibition, anti-emetic, prokinetic1-3g dried ginger/dayStrong for nausea, good for OA painWarfarin (mild antiplatelet)
🟡 Herb Safety Before Surgery
Stop all herbal medicines 2 weeks before elective surgery. Garlic, ginger, ginkgo, ginseng — antiplatelet effects. Hawthorn — cardiac effects. St John's Wort — CYP induction affects anaesthetic metabolism. Valerian, kava — potentiate anaesthetic agents. Always take a full herbal history at pre-operative assessment — most patients do not volunteer this information unless specifically asked.

cards.title: Hepatoprotective Herbs — Liver Support

rows: Antioxidant (↑ GSH), membrane stabiliser, anti-fibrotic, ↑ protein synthesis
↑ Bile flow, mild diuretic, prebiotic, anti-inflammatory

pearls.label: 🟡 Curcumin bioavailability — critical
pearls.text: Curcumin has only 1% oral bioavailability as standard powder. Solutions: (1) Piperine (black pepper extract, bioperine) 20mg increases absorption 2000%, (2) Liposomal/phospholipid complex (Meriva, BCM-95) 7–29× increase, (3) Nanoparticle formulations. Always specify the formulation when recommending. BCM-95 or Meriva at 500mg/day equals 8–10g standard curcumin powder.

cards._legacyHtml:

Dandelion rootTaraxacin, inulin, bitter sesquiterpenes↑ Bile flow, mild diuretic, prebiotic, anti-inflammatoryLiver congestion, constipation, UTI4–10g root/day or tincture
Globe artichokeCynarin, luteolin↑ Bile production/flow, hepatoprotective, lipid-loweringDyslipidaemia ★, NAFLD, dyspepsia320–640mg extract/day
Turmeric / CurcuminCurcuminoidsNF-κB ↓, COX-2 ↓, Nrf2 ↑, anti-fibroticNAFLD ★, IBD, arthritis, cancer adjunct500–2000mg/day + piperine or liposomal
AndrographisAndrographolideAnti-viral, NF-κB ↓, immune modulationViral hepatitis, URTI, IBD200–400mg andrographolide/day
🟡 Curcumin bioavailability — critical
Curcumin has only 1% oral bioavailability as standard powder. Solutions: (1) Piperine (black pepper extract, bioperine) 20mg increases absorption 2000%, (2) Liposomal/phospholipid complex (Meriva, BCM-95) 7–29× increase, (3) Nanoparticle formulations. Always specify the formulation when recommending. BCM-95 or Meriva at 500mg/day equals 8–10g standard curcumin powder.

cards.title: Nervines & Neurological Herbs

rows: GABA-A potentiation (similar to benzos, no dependence)

pearls.label: 🟡 Lion's Mane & NGF
pearls.text: Hericium erinaceus (Lion's Mane) is the only known food that stimulates NGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis in the brain. NGF promotes neuroplasticity, remyelination, and neurogenesis. RCT evidence for mild cognitive impairment (Mori et al., 2009 — 50% cognitive improvement vs placebo over 16 weeks). Requires 8 weeks minimum. Use hot-water extract (fruiting body) not mycelium for NGF-stimulating hericenones and erinacines.

cards._legacyHtml:

ValerianSedative nervineGABA transaminase inhibition, GABA-A agonismInsomnia ★, anxiety, muscle tension400–900mg 30min before bed
PassionflowerAnxiolytic nervineGABA-A potentiation (similar to benzos, no dependence)Anxiety ★, insomnia, withdrawal support250–500mg or tincture
Lemon balmRelaxing nervineMAO inhibition (mild), GABA-T inhibition, antiviralAnxiety, insomnia, cold sores, ADHD (children)300–600mg (or tea)
Bacopa monnieriNootropic↑ Acetylcholine, antioxidant, ↑ BDNF, adaptogenicMemory ★, ADHD adjunct, cognitive ageing300mg/day standardised (must take 8–12 weeks)
Lion's ManeNootropic mushroomNGF (nerve growth factor) stimulation, remyelinationCognitive decline ★, neuropathy, depression500–3000mg/day extract
🟡 Lion's Mane & NGF
Hericium erinaceus (Lion's Mane) is the only known food that stimulates NGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis in the brain. NGF promotes neuroplasticity, remyelination, and neurogenesis. RCT evidence for mild cognitive impairment (Mori et al., 2009 — 50% cognitive improvement vs placebo over 16 weeks). Requires 8 weeks minimum. Use hot-water extract (fruiting body) not mycelium for NGF-stimulating hericenones and erinacines.
